tabs.title: How to use this sheet
grid: PLEASE DO NOT WRITE IN THIS- DOWNLOAD AND MAKE A COPY AND WRITE IN YOUR OWN COPY
Step 1: Click file at the top left of the screen and scoll to the fourth option ' make a copy'
Step 2: Your copy will pop up and allow you to work in the google sheet in your own google drive.
Step 1: You can also download the sheet by clicking file and scoll down to the seventh option 'download'
Step 2: Pick your prefered downloading file and use at your own leisure.

tabs.title: Why Tracking Matters
grid: Why Tracking Your Weights Actually Matters
You can't manage what you don't measure.
If you don't know what you lifted last week, how do you know if you're getting stronger? Tracking gives you a clear, honest record of where you've been - and proof of how far you've come.
It keeps you progressing, not just showing up.

There's a big difference between going through the motions and actually training with intention. When you track your weights, you give yourself a target to beat. Even adding 2.5lbs to a lift is a win - and those wins add up fast over an 8-week phase.
It takes the guesswork out of your sessions.
No more trying to remember what you squatted three weeks ago. You walk in, check your sheet, and know exactly where to start. That means less wasted energy and more focused effort where it counts.
Your trainer can push you, but at the end of the day, you're the one holding the pen. Tracking puts you in the driver's seat of your own progress. It's your story, written in reps and pounds.
And at the end of this phase? We're putting it all to the test.

We have a 1RM testing event coming up, plus our 300/500 Club - a combined total across your Squat, Bench, Deadlift, and Hip Thrust. If you haven't been tracking, you won't know how close you are. If you have been tracking, you might just surprise yourself.

tabs.title: 1RM Testing
grid: 1RM Testing - Estimated One Rep Max (Epley Formula)
Weight will calculate automatically for you once you add your weight and reps
Your combined 1RM total across Squat + Bench + Deadlift + Hip Thrust. Hit 300lbs to join the 300 Club - hit 500lbs for the elite 500 Club!
